authors: Devulder J, De Laat M, Van Bastelaere M, Rolly G
title: Spinal Cord Stimulation: A Valuable Treatment for Chronic Failed Back Surgery Patients
journal_name: Journal of Pain and Symptom Management
study_question: Are long-term outcomes and costs favorable for SCS in chronic failed back surgery syndrome?
population_assessed: 69 chronic failed back surgery syndrome patients implanted with spinal cord stimulators
follow_up_duration: 4.9 years mean use; study period 13 years
outcome_measures: pain relief, return to work, drug intake, stimulator use, annual costs
inclusion_criteria: CFBSS, no curative surgery option, no psychological impairment, successful 2-week trial stimulation
answer_to_study_question: SCS provided sustained pain relief for many patients and may be cost-effective when other therapies fail.
